concepts.title: MySQL GROUP BY Clause
concepts.description: The GROUP BY clause is used in conjunction with the aggregate functions to group the result-set by one or more columns.

concepts.title: MySQL Aggregate Functions
concepts.description: MySQL provides various aggregate functions like SUM, COUNT, MAX, MIN, AVG to perform calculations on a set of values.

concepts.title: GROUP BY vs HAVING
concepts.description: GROUP BY is used to group the result-set by one or more columns, whereas HAVING is used to filter groups based on conditions.

concepts.title: ACID
concepts.description: A mnemonic to remember the properties of MySQL database: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.

concepts.title: Incorrect Use of GROUP BY
concepts.description: Students often use GROUP BY without specifying the columns to group by, leading to incorrect results or errors.

concepts.title: MySQL User-Defined Functions
concepts.description: MySQL allows users to create their own functions using the CREATE FUNCTION statement, which can be used to simplify complex queries.

concepts.title: GROUP BY Syntax
concepts.description: The basic syntax of the GROUP BY clause is: GROUP BY column1, column2, ..., columnN;

concepts.title: Aggregate Function Characteristics
concepts.description: Students should recall the characteristics of aggregate functions, such as ignoring NULL values, and how to handle NULL values in queries.
